messages.content: which of the following is true or the federalists? select all that apply A. two of their leaders were john adams and alexander hamilton B. they believed that the federal government should possess all powers not specifically denied to it C. they set the nations financial affairs in order and encourages manufacturing and trade D. they served the interests of wealthy, propertied White American men

A. Two of their leaders were John Adams and Alexander Hamilton

B. They believed that the federal government should possess all powers not specifically denied to it

C. They set the nation's financial affairs in order and encouraged manufacturing and trade

D. They served the interests of wealthy, propertied White American men

before the constitution was even written, two parties vying for a direction for the United States. name them and their characteristics A. the federalists wanted a strong federal government, and the republcians wanted freer elections B. the antifederalists were against a strong government and the whigs wanted states to have more power C. the whigs wanted a standing militia and the federalists wanted a federal reserve D. the federalists wanted  a strong centralized government, and the antifederalists wanted to restrict the federal government and give states more freedoms and legal control

A. The Federalists wanted a strong federal government, and the Republicans wanted freer elections.
B. The Antifederalists were against a strong government, and the Whigs wanted states to have more power.
D. The Federalists wanted a strong centralized government, and the Antifederalists wanted to restrict the federal government and give states more freedoms and legal control.
choose only one.
The Federalists wanted a strong centralized government, and the Antifederalists wanted to restrict the federal government and give states more freedoms and legal control.
what are the major american political parties? select all that apply A. democrats B. liberals C. conservatives D. republicans

A. Democrats
C. Conservatives
D. Republicans

which word goes with "limits the federal government to powers the constitution specifically delegated to it A. constitutional convention B. provision C. alien and sedition acts D. strict construction E. loose construction F. faction
D. strict construction

which word goes with "gathering for the purpose of creating a constitution to lay down the laws for running the US" A. constitutional convention B. provision C. alien and sedition acts D. strict construction E. loose construction F. faction
A. constitutional convention

which word goes with "arrangement or groundwork laid to get a program going" A. constitutional convention B. provision C. alien and sedition acts D. strict construction E. loose construction F. faction
B. provision

which word goes with "legislation giving the government power to deport non-citizens and arrest critics of the government" A. constitutional convention B. provision C. alien and sedition acts D. strict construction E. loose construction F. faction
C. alien and sedition acts

which word goes with "allowing the federal government all powers not denied it within the constitution" A. constitutional convention B. provision C. alien and sedition acts D. strict construction E. loose construction F. faction
E. loose construction

which of the following is NOT true of the anti-federalists A. they did not want the federal government to have a standing army or tax them to pay off the national debt incurred by the revolutionary war B. they did not want a federal government that would allow states too much power C. they were led by land-owning Thomas Jefferson who wanted a weak central government D. they were largely comprised of southern land-owners who did not want northern trade and commerce to take over the country

C. They were led by land-owning Thomas Jefferson who wanted a weak central government.

The statement is not true because Thomas Jefferson was actually a prominent leader of the Democratic-Republican Party and advocated for a limited federal government, but not necessarily a weak central government. The Democratic-Republicans (also known as Republicans) believed in a strict interpretation of the Constitution and strong state governments.
the _ believed that the federal government should possess all powers not specifically denied to it A. federalists B. anti-federalists
A. Federalists

the democratic split was so severe during the 1860 elections that A. they ran two candidates for the presidency B. the majority of the members joined the republican C. they had no candidates running for the presidential election D. the party disbanded
A. They ran two candidates for the presidency
the _party is the oldest political party in the united states A. republican B. whig C. democratic
C. Democratic
the anti-federalist would limit the federal government strictly to A. the powers the constitution delegated to it B. coining of money C. making International treaties D. managing of foreign affairs
A. The powers the constitution delegated to it

which statements are true? select all that apply A. the democrats won the presidency in the 90's but lost control of the congress B. the anti-federalists were one of the first two American political parties; they wanted a strong central government C. the democrats blamed republicans for the stock market crash in 1929 D. the era of good feelings was a period from 1816-1824 where there was only one political party: the democrat-republicans E. the democratic party won considerable political strength during the civil war

A. The Democrats won the presidency in the 90's but lost control of the congress.
D. The Era of Good Feelings was a period from 1816-1824 where there was only one political party: the Democrat-Republicans.
E. The Democratic Party won considerable political strength during the Civil War.
which of the following are democratic presidents? select all that apply A. Wilson B. Reagan C. Nixon D. Kennedy E. Johnson

A. Wilson
D. Kennedy
E. Johnson
which of the following are republican presidents? select all that apply A. eisenhower B. lincoln C. bush D. clinton E. wilson

A. Eisenhower
B. Lincoln
C. Bush
the democrats fought almost bitterly at times over A. banking policies B. slavery issues C. tariff rates D. all of the above
D. All of the above
who was praised for his efforts to improve medicare but was criticized for starting a failed war in Iraq A. george w bush B. bill clinton C. ronald reagan
A. George W. Bush

which was unusual about Hillary Clinton's loss in the 2016 election A. she was only the fifth candidate to win the popular vote but lose the electoral college vote B. she lost because former first ladies are legally not allowed to become president C. donald trump lost the electoral college vote but overwhelmingly won the popular vote D. donald trump won after requesting a recount in flordia and other key states
A. She was only the fifth candidate to win the popular vote but lose the electoral college vote

which of the following best describes republican dominance from the time of abraham lincoln? A. its politices appealed to a wide variety of constituents, including farmers, merchants, and industrialists B. the american public generally didn't want to continue enslaving people C. jane addams helped theodore roosevelt split the democratic vote 1912 D. there was general economic security during republican presidencies
D. There was general economic security during Republican presidencies

which of the following are republican presidents? select all that apply A. dwight d eisenhower B. william howard taft C. john f kennedy D. lyndon b johnson E. ulysses s grant F. ronald reagan G. theodore roosevelt

A. Dwight D Eisenhower
B. William Howard Taft
E. Ulysses S Grant
F. Ronald Reagan
G. Theodore Roosevelt

throughout the 20th century republicans have done which of the following, select all that apply A. lost to jimmy carter and won with ronald reagan B. elected dwight d eisenhower-twice C. had their strongest showing with ronald regan who won all but one state D. mostly lost to democrats in landslide defeats E. won majorities in both the house, senate, and presidency during the middle of the century

A. Lost to Jimmy Carter and won with Ronald Reagan 
B. Elected Dwight D Eisenhower - twice 
C. Had their strongest showing with Ronald Reagan who won all but one state 
E. Won majorities in both the House, Senate, and presidency during the middle of the century

why did divisions in the republican party occur A. because there was a majority in both house and senate B. because conservative republicans re-elected Taft at the partys 1912 national convention C. due to the landslide defeat to lyndon johnson D. because the national progressive party was formed by a woman
B. Because conservative Republicans re-elected Taft at the party's 1912 national convention
what word goes with "having the larger number in the house of congress assuring strong voting power in decision of legislation" A. challenger B. incumbent C. majorities D. landslide defeat
C. Majorities
what word goes with “losing by a sizable margin in an election” A. challenger B. incumbent C. majorities D. landslide defeat
D. landslide defeat
what word goes with “a person who currently holds an office” A. challenger B. incumbent C. majorities D. landslide defeat
B. incumbent
the republican party originated A. from a firm political stance against enslavement B. in the whig party C. in the southern states D. with abraham lincoln as the first candidate
B. in the Whig party

a significant irregularity in the 2000 elections was the fact that A. george w bush won by the smallest victory in history B. flordia conceded the elections to vice president gore C. a sitting democratic president was defeated by the republican challenger D. the election results were challenged in court up to five weeks after the electio
D. the election results were challenged in court up to five weeks after the election
the populist party of the 1890's was an example of a party formed chiefly to help a A. candidate register in a national election B. land area achieve statehood C. specific group of people
C. specific group of people
the _ party is a left-wing protest party that was formed in 1919 A. libertarian B. american communist C. federalist D. whig
B. American Communist
the _party is an example of a single-issue party A. republican B. progressive C. socialist labor D. prohibition
D. Prohibition

which are examples of left-wing protest groups? select multiple answers A. populist party B. progressive party C. communist party USA D. socialist labor party E. libertarian party F. socialist workers party G. american independent party H. democratic socialists of america

B. Progressive Party
C. Communist Party USA
D. Socialist Labor Party
F. Socialist Workers Party
H. Democratic Socialists of America

which parties began by splitting away from two major political parties? select all that apply A. liberal republicans B. democratic socialists of america C. dixiecrats D. roosevelt progressives E. constitution

A. Liberal Republicans
C. Dixiecrats
D. Roosevelt Progressives
the _ way is to  strongly oppose any government interference into americans personal family, and businesses A. liberal republican B. gold democratic C. dixiecrat D. libertarian
D. Libertarian
farmers burdened with debt founded which parties in the late 1800s select all that apply A. gold democrats B. progressive C. populist D. greenback

C. Populist
D. Greenback
the _ stands for peach and non-violence, ecological wisdom, grassroots democracy and social justice A. american communist party B. populist party C. socialist party D. green party
D. Green Party

the prohibition party was founded in 1869 solely for the purpose of preventing the manufacutirng and sale alcoholic beverages in the united states. the prohibition party is an example of A. a party that has a broad program and attempts to gain national favor B. a party that has only one goal C. a group that broke away from a major political party
B. A party that has only one goal
the party formed by texas billonaire ross perot in the 1990s was the united we stand, or _ party A. libertarian B. perot C. new D. reform
D. Reform
